entry.title: Crystal Report Runtime, trying to run report from vb.net website
entry.content:
I am trying to run a Crystal Report using Crystal Report Runtime in my vb.net website.  When the page loads into the browser, the report doesn't open.  I get no messages or errors, just an empty browser.  It behaves the same when I test it locally and on the server.  Why doesn't the runtime load the report?

Here's my source code in the .aspx file:

<%@ Page Language="vb" AutoEventWireup="false" CodeBehind="PrintEmergencyRpt.aspx.vb" Inherits="Security_Badge_Requests.PrintEmergencyRpt" %>

<%@ Register assembly="CrystalDecisions.Web, Version=13.0.4000.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=692fbea5521e1304" namespace="CrystalDecisions.Web" tagprefix="CR" %>

<!DOCTYPE html>

<html xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1999/xhtml">
<head runat="server">
<title>Crystal Report Viewer</title>
<style type="text/css">
.auto-style1 {}
</style>
</head>
<body>
<form id="form1" runat="server">
<div>
<CR:CrystalReportViewer ID="CrystalReportViewer1" runat="server" AutoDataBind="true" ReportSourceID="CrystalReportSource1" />
<CR:CrystalReportSource ID="CrystalReportSource1" runat="server">
<Report FileName="~\AccessGrants-Emergency_Response.rpt">
</Report>
</CR:CrystalReportSource>
<br />
<asp:Button ID="Button1" runat="server" CssClass="auto-style1" Text="Button" Width="109px" />
<br />
</div>
</form>
</body>
</html>

 

Here's the code in the aspx.vb file:

Imports CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine
Imports CrystalDecisions.Shared


Public Class PrintEmergencyRpt
Inherits System.Web.UI.Page

Protected Sub Page_init(ByVal sender As Object, ByVal e As System.EventArgs) Handles Me.Init
Dim tbCurrent1 As CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine.Table
Dim tliCurrent1 As CrystalDecisions.Shared.TableLogOnInfo
Dim cr As New CrystalDecisions.CrystalReports.Engine.ReportDocument()

Try
'Map the actual path on the server to the report file
Dim strPath As String = Server.MapPath("AccessGrants-Emergency_Response.rpt")
'Load the report
cr.Load(strPath, CrystalDecisions.Shared.OpenReportMethod.OpenReportByTempCopy)

For Each tbCurrent1 In cr.Database.Tables
tliCurrent1 = tbCurrent1.LogOnInfo
With (tliCurrent1.ConnectionInfo)
.UserID = "xxxxx"
.Password = "xxxxxxxxx"
End With
tbCurrent1.ApplyLogOnInfo(tliCurrent1)
Next tbCurrent1

'Set the source for the report
CrystalReportViewer1.ReportSource = cr
Catch ex As Exception
Response.Write("The Following Error has occurred: " & ex.InnerException.ToString)
End Try

cr = Nothing
End Sub

Here are the added references and the file version:

Please help, I have tried different code from AI, but nothing works.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Join in DataSphere
entry.content:
Hello @Ganjos 

On the first glance I cannot recognize your joining key fields... but since you are using an inner join and you cannot see any results, it means that there are no matching keys in both tables.

If you want to "attach" the data from one table to the other, you need a left outer join.

Or you need to verify that the keys, which you want to join have the same data type and length.

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI IP Ranges for Cloud Foundry Environment
entry.content: To allow SCC to connect to BTP, it needs to have access to the hosts listed in the Network section of the Prerequisites according to your region. i.e., the IP allow-listing mechanism (Firewall, proxy, etc.) on the Cloud Connector server should allow outbound (to BTP) traffic towards the listed host / IP addresses in the document. The port number is 443. There is no need to open port in the firewall for inbound (from BTP) requests.

entry.title: Set-up EDI connection - 2EL and 4A1
entry.content:
Hello,

 

The client wants to set up an EDI connection. We found 2 scope items that can do so, namely 2EL ( Integration of Sales with External Buyers )  and 4A1 (Automation of Order-to-Invoice with SAP Business Network ).  The difference is that scope item 4A1 uses SAP Business Network, while 2EL does not use this.

In the set-up guide of 2EL it is mentioned to create communication scenario SAP_COM_0223. However, in the set-up guide of 4A1 it is mentioned that 'This procedure describes how to deactivate the Sales with Buyers Integration (SAP_COM_0223) communication arrangement in order to set up the Sales with SAP Business Network Integration (SAP_COM_0848) communication arrangement instead.'.

However, some customers of the client have a connection to SAP Business Network, while other customers of the client do not have this. This result in the issue that we want both scope items to run. Is this possible? Or is it only possible to have 1 of the 2 scope items running at the same time?

entry.title: SAP Fiori development newsletter March 2025 (issue #33)
entry.content:
AI-assisted application development continues to be a big focus for SAP. Some of the prototypes we have been working on over the past several months are now part of our released software. We are seeing new innovations that blur the lines between no-code, low-code, and pro-code. This issue also has articles on the fundamentals: Excel, table controls, and CAP.

For ongoing updates, I encourage you to join our monthly SAP Fiori development roundtable. You can meet online with fellow developers and the SAP engineers who build our professional development tools. Register online or email me for an invitation. Our next meeting is Wednesday, February 12, 2025. 

If you prefer to receive this in your inbox, please subscribe to the SAP Fiori development newsletter.

Development News

Overview of all GenAI options in SAP Build Code

SAP Build Code includes many GenAI capabilities through Joule. This blog post summarizes the current features, discusses the variety of use cases they support, and provides recommendations that are specific to the new functionality.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/overview-of-all-genai-options-in-sap-build-code/ba-p/13984474

UI design and technology for web and mobile

This post covers how to build Joule agents, how AI makes it easier to develop UIs, for both SAP Fiori elements and SAP UI5 freestyle apps, and how to create an entire application from a sketch: data models in the backend, services, and the UI. Beyond AI, it discusses updates for web and mobile UI design and development.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-user-experience-q1-2025-update-part-7-ui-design-and-technology-for-web/ba-p/14034290

Simple table maintenance in SAP Fiori using UI5 Smart Controls

Switching from the “classic” SAP GUI table maintenance to an SAP Fiori-based solution doesn’t have to be complex. By using a CAP or RAP Backend, a freestyle UI5 app, and OData V2 (plus UI annotations), you can quickly set up a user-friendly, fully responsive table maintenance application.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/simple-table-maintenance-in-sap-fiori-using-ui5-smart-controls/ba-p/14014547

Visualizing your SAP bill of materials using CAP, SAP Fiori elements, and the UI5 network graph

This post demonstrates how to create a simple Bill of Materials (BOM) relationship visualization using SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) and consume it in an SAP Fiori elements application. It explains how to display the BOM structure as a Network Graph, making it easy for end users to navigate parent/child relationships among materials.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/visualizing-your-sap-bill-of-materials-using-cap-fiori-elements-and-the-ui5/ba-p/14025612

Deprecation of karma-ui5 plugin

SAPUI5 provides many testing options, such as unit and integration tests, which are summarized in UI5 Testing. As the Karma test runner is deprecated since April 2023, the UI5 team now has to deprecate karma-ui5.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/deprecation-of-karma-ui5-plugin/ba-p/13954060

SAP Build Code updates

SAP Build Code continues to evolve with improvements to RFCs, inline editing capability for CDS entities, and much more. Read about the latest updates.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/product-updates-for-sap-build-code-january-2025-edition/ba-p/13993021

AI is NOT invited to our meetings (friendly reminder)

In our monthly SAP development roundtable several people have been recording our calls and generating AI summaries. We have received complaints. Going forward, we ask that you don’t do this. Our goal is for everyone to share openly. Having a recording and/or transcript might prevent this. If we demo something new and exciting, we’ll create a recording to share. Otherwise, please do not invite your AI or chatbot to join. People only!

Events

SAP Fiori Innovation Day Silicon Valley recap

SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Silicon Valley was an exciting day of learning, networking, demos, food, and fun. Read about what you missed. Hope you can join us next time.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/sap-fiori-innovation-day-in-silicon-valley-highlighted-how-ai-makes-app/ba-p/14011083

We are planning more SAP Fiori Innovation Day events in 2025

Most likely locations are Brno, Dallas, and maybe Paris. We'll have details in this newsletter when we finalize the dates and open the registration page.

 

Back issues from the past year

SAP Fiori development newsletter January 2025 (issue #32)

SAP Fiori development newsletter November 2024 (issue #31)

SAP Fiori development newsletter September 2024 (issue #30)

SAP Fiori development newsletter July 2024 (issue #29)

SAP FIori development newsletter May 2024 (issue #28)

SAP Fiori development newsletter March 2024 (issue #27)

entry.title: Automatic Invoice creation for EWM related Deliveries
entry.content:
Backend System – SAP S/4HANA 2020FP02

Overview  -

This blog offers comprehensive guidance on implementing automatic invoicing for scenarios related to Extended Warehouse Management (EWM).

Business Scenario

Initially, the business was using storage location-based stock management, incorporating automatic invoicing through a custom solution at the point of goods issue for the delivery document. However, with the implementation of Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), this enhancement extension failed to function as intended, hence, the real-time invoice creation process was interrupted. We were unable to identify an appropriate user exit to implement this enhancement. Consequently, the Goods Issue status of the delivery was not completed when the existing enhancement for the invoice creation program was executed, resulting in the failure of invoice creation.

The VF04 background schedule was proposed to the business. However, the business did not agree due to potential delays in invoice creation and the need to identify the invoice creator as the same user who performed the PGI. Using VF04 would result in the invoice creator being marked as the background user.

 

 

Step 1

In order to maintain output condition records based on the warehouse number, it is essential to include the LGNUM-ERP warehouse in the delivery output field catalog. Once this field is added, it will be available for creating a condition table and subsequently included in an access sequence. This enhancement ensures that the solution is restricted to Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) locations.

 

  1. Include field ZZLGNUM in communication structure KOMKBV2. This structure contains an Include that is provided for additional fields. Add the field with the name ZZLGNUM in Include KOMKBZ4.
  2. In structure KOMB in Include KOMBZ, you have to add the same field (same procedure as for item 1).
  3. In view V_T681F, you must create the same field with transaction SM31 under key B V2.
  4. 4. Program LVCOMU04 contains Perform USEREXIT_KOMKBV2_FILL. This Perform is in program RVCOMFZZ.
  5.  Modify the source code as follows: 

 

FORM_USEREXIT_KOMKBV2_FILL.

COM_KBV2-ZZLGNUM = COM_LIKP-LGNUM.

ENDFORM.

 

Step 2

 

Create an output condition table utilizing the Delivery Type and Warehouse Number (ZZLGNUM).

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Step 3

 

Create an access sequence using the previously created table to assign it to the condition type.

 

 

 

 

Step 4

Delivery Output Condition type creation.

 

 

Program – /SPE/DLV_IBGI_PROCESSING

Form Routine – ENTRY

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Step 5

 

Create a requirement to be used for the delivery output determination procedure.

Go to VOFM -> Requirements -> Output control

 

Create a new routine copying routine 1 code and add a wait for 2 seconds. This wait will make sure that the delivery is completed before the invoice is created. Otherwise, the invoice will be shown in the document flow before the PGI entry.

 

 

 

Maintain the Application as ‘V2’ and activate the routine.

 

Run the ‘RV80HGEN’ program using the SE38 transaction to activate the routine.

 

Note: This is client-specific activation.

 

 

Step 6

 

Create a new Output determination procedure with the newly created output type.

 

 

 

 

 

Assign the created requirement 901 to the procedure. This routine assignment ensures that invoice creation is delayed until after the PGI entry is created. It will make sure that the output type is assigned to the delivery only after the PGI is completed, preventing invoice generation from happening before the PGI entry.

 

 

Step 7

Assign the delivery output procedure to the Delivery type.

 

 

 

 

 

Step 8

 

Output condition maintain

Go to VV21 in order to maintain the output condition for the ZREA output type.

 

 

 

 

 

 

Output type ZREA will be available after the Goods Issue has been posted. Consequently, the invoice related to the post-goods issue will be created automatically.

 

Conclusion –

This configuration and the developments will ensure that invoice creation will be automated without major customization for the SAP standard solution.

 

Special thanks to the Altria Consulting team @Kalana_Chathuranga_Herath, @HasiniTW, @Nilupul_Rambukwelle, @Dushan_Sasmitha and @Ravindu_Herath.

entry.title: ICMR - Manage Assignments App - How to add total/selected Amount to Email template
entry.content:
Hello,

 

In Manage Assignments Fiori App we can send email via Communication -> Send Email. In the email, a table is included with all the rows which were selected from Leading Unit/ Parter Unit Unassigned. If rows are selected from both sections, two tables are included in the email with all selected rows.

The standard email template which is used is ICA_INTERCOMPANY.

How can I add to a custom email template (similar to the standard one) a total row in the table which would sum up the amount (WSL) of the selected rows? If a new line in the table is not possible - as in the template email we just have {{ICADOCM}} as table - can I add somehow at least the total amount below the table?

 

Thank you,

Kind Regards,

Alina

entry.title: Re: To implement SEO settings in a Career Site Builder or any website builder:
entry.content: Making sure your Career Site Builder pages are search-friendly is key, especially if you're looking to improve marketing Suffolk UK. Make sure your job postings have clear, descriptive titles and URLs with relevant keywords. Use the "Additional Content" sections for custom meta descriptions and H1 headings. Also, compress images to improve speed and check that your pages are mobile-friendly—Google values that a lot for rankings.

entry.title: 🚀 Mastering PAS-C01: My Journey with Official Resources and P2PCerts
entry.content:
As I prepare to take the PAS-C01 (AWS Certified: SAP on AWS - Specialty) exam, I wanted to share my strategy. I combined official AWS resources with P2PCerts' practice questions, which helped me thoroughly cover essential topics like AWS Snow Family, DataSync, Storage Gateway, and FSx for Windows. The technical depth and real-world scenarios in P2PCerts' material were invaluable.

Now, as I gear up for the exam, I’d love to hear any pro tips or last-minute strategies from those who’ve already aced it. What should I focus on in the final days? Your advice could be the key to my success! 🙌

entry.title: Crossboarding process (internal hire process) should trigger from Recruitment Management Only
entry.content:
Crossboarding process (internal hire process) should trigger from Recruitment Management and restrict to trigger from employee central

Introduction

We would like to share our experiences of using Crossboarding process (Internal Hire Process) should trigger for internal employees only from Recruitment Management meanwhile should restrict to trigger from Employee central. It provides solution to complex client requirements for trigger crossboarding process.

In general, as per the standard functional in SuccessFactors, Crossboarding process will trigger from Recruitment Management and as well as from Employee Central to Onboarding 2.0.

Here, we are presenting you how to trigger Crossboarding process from Recruitment Management to Onboarding 2.0 only and meanwhile will show how to restrict to trigger Crossboarding process from Employee Central to Onboarding 2.0. This blog will help you address similar requirements in your projects.

Trends/Problem Statement / Overview

Scenario 1: The Crossboarding process should trigger from Recruitment Management to  Onboarding 2.0.

Scenario 2: The Crossboarding process should restrict to trigger from Employee central to Onboarding 2.0.

Challenges

As per the current SuccessFactors system design, the primary challenge is that we are unable to control the restriction of internal hire records in the Onboarding dashboard even when transfer events are restricted from business rules, the Onboarding dashboard will still display empty transfer event records when transferring from Employee Central as shown below.

Solution

The following steps will trigger Crossboarding process from Recruitment Management to Onboarding 2.0 and as well as restrict to trigger from Employee central to Onboarding 2.0.

  • We can clone an existing process variant and modify the existing process flow by adding process steps to suit customer requirement.
  • Create custom Field “Source System for Onboarding” in Job Information portlet.

  • The following business rule pick the Crossboarding process variant after initiate the onboarding process from RCM to Onboarding 2.0

  • The following business rule restrict to trigger crossboarding process from Employee Central to Onboarding 2.0.

  • The following business trigger Crossboarding process based on the event reasons derived in the business rule.

  • The following business rules need to be place under Job Information in Manage business configuration. Go to Manage Business Configuration, under HRIS Elements select jobInfo. Scroll down to the trigger rules section and set the Transfer rule for the Event Type as onPostSave.

Expected Output / Outcome

Scenario 1:  As soon as initiated Crossboarding from RCM, The Crossboarding process initiated successfully and can see new hire record in Onboarding dashboard.

Scenario 2: As soon as initiated Crossboarding from Employee Central, The Crossboarding process will not trigger, and internal hire record will not appear in Onboarding dashboard.

2.Benefits

It provides solution to complex client requirements to configure crossboarding process. It will help to restrict to trigger Crossboarding from Employee Central.

It provides solutions to trigger specific transfer events can trigger from Recruitment Management to Onboarding 2.0 and can trigger specific transfer events within employee central module (for example trigger promotion event reasons from Recruitment Management to Onboarding 2.0 and demotion event reasons trigger only in Employee central).

The main benefits are as follows:

  • Hiring Manager / Responsible person can review the only specific event reasons internal hire data during Onboarding process.
  • By restricting the trigger of specific event reasons in the internal hire process from Employee Central, manual intervention of the Hiring Manager/Responsible Persons' efforts during the Onboarding process can be reduced.

3.Conclusion

In general, as per the standard functional in SuccessFactors, Crossboarding process will trigger from Recruitment Management and as well as from Employee Central to Onboarding 2.0. This document will help to the customers who don’t need the Crossboarding process to trigger from Employee central. The detailed configuration steps of this document will helpful how to trigger Crossboarding process from RCM to Onboarding 2.0 only and meanwhile will show how to restrict to trigger Crossboarding process from Employee Central to Onboarding 2.0.

The customizable nature of SAP SuccessFactors allows organizations to tailor the Crossboarding processes to their specific requirements.

SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding

entry.title: Auto Submission and Approval Setup of Employee Timesheet: A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content:
Introduction

Approval is a critical component of any time management system. With SuccessFactors, you can configure approvals and submissions for Employee Timesheets in multiple ways to fit diverse client needs. In this post, we’ll explore a complex scenario involving timesheet submission and approval setup—demonstrating how to leverage SuccessFactors configurations to automate processes for fully integrated timesheets while ensuring manual amendments trigger the standard approval workflow.

Customer Requirement

In this scenario, the customer has implemented several SuccessFactors Time Management modules; Time Off, Time Sheet, and Time Tracking - while still using a third-party Time Registration system for a large group of employees.

Key points include:

  • Integrated Time Registration: For these employees, all time registration starts in the third-party system. Both absence and attendance data are integrated into SuccessFactors.
  • Amendments in SuccessFactors: Although the third-party system pre-approves all time data, any amendments made directly in SuccessFactors should trigger a standard submission and approval workflow.
  • Dual Data Sources:
    • Integrated Data: Time sheets that only have imported entries require no additional submission or approval.
    • Manual Data: Time sheets with manual entries (or a mix of integrated and manual entries) must follow the standard submission and approval process.

Scenarios

The following scenarios outline how timesheet submissions are handled:

  1. Time Sheet with Only Integrated Data:
    • Automatically submitted and approved.
  2. Time Sheet with Only Manual Data:
    • Follows the standard submission and approval process.
  3. Time Sheet with Both Integrated and Manual Data:
    • Also follows the standard submission and approval process.

The Solution

To meet the requirements, several configuration steps were implemented, covering both submission and approval processes.

 

Submission Process

A Time Recording Submission Configuration was created and assigned to the Time Recording Profile.

 

 

This configuration includes a Submission Rule that:

  • Checks the Entry Source: The business rule inspects entries in the Employee Time Sheet object.
  • Determines the Process:
    • If the timesheet contains only imported entries, it is automatically submitted.
    • If any manual entries exist, the timesheet must be submitted manually.

Approval Process

The standard workflow for timesheets is managed through a Save Rule on the Employee Time Sheet object as follows:

This rule ensures that:

  • Submission Check: It first verifies whether the timesheet has been submitted and is awaiting approval.
  • Manual Entry Verification:
    • If manual entries are detected, the standard workflow (as defined in the Time Recording Profile) is triggered.
    • If no manual entries are found, the workflow is bypassed, and the timesheet is automatically approved.

Testing

Automatic timesheet submission is managed by the TLMTriggerTasks job in Provisioning. When this job runs, it triggers the following processes sequentially:

  1. Trigger Recalculation Job
  2. Import External Times Job
  3. Generate Working Times Job
  4. Submit Time Sheets Job

Since TLMTriggerTasks typically runs on a daily schedule, it is recommended to manually run the Submit Time Sheets Job during testing. This allows you to validate that:

  • Timesheets with only integrated entries are automatically submitted and approved.
  • Timesheets with any manual data trigger the standard submission and approval workflow.

Conclusion

By configuring specific submission and approval rules within SuccessFactors, organizations can automate the processing of timesheets containing only integrated data while ensuring that any modifications or manual entries follow a robust approval workflow. This setup not only streamlines operations but also provides the flexibility to handle varied data sources and user inputs.

If you have any questions or would like to share your own experiences with configuring timesheet approvals in SuccessFactors, please feel free to connect and start a conversation.

entry.title: Performance Rating Considering Percentage of the Multiple Manager's Rating
entry.content:
Here's how we achieved this:

  1. Understanding the Requirement: Our client emphasized the need to balance the appraisal process by incorporating evaluations from both the manager and a second manager. This was essential to ensure that the final rating was not biased and provided a more holistic view of the employee's performance.
  2. Defining the Weightage: We began by determining the appropriate weightage for each evaluator. After discussions and analysis, it was decided that the primary manager's evaluation would contribute to 60% of the overall rating, while the second manager's evaluation would make up the remaining 40%. This ratio was chosen based on the roles and responsibilities of the managers and their interactions with the employees.
  3. Creating the Evaluation Criteria: Both managers were provided with a standardized evaluation form, which included various performance metrics such as job knowledge, quality of work, communication skills, teamwork, and punctuality. Each criterion had a specific scoring range, allowing for detailed and consistent assessments.
  4. Implementing the Rating Calculation: We developed an automated system to calculate the final rating. The system aggregated the scores from both managers according to the predefined weightage. For instance, if the primary manager gave a score of 80 out of 100 and the second manager gave a score of 85 out of 100, the final rating would be calculated as follows:
    • Primary Manager's Contribution: 80 * 0.60 = 48
    • Second Manager's Contribution: 85 * 0.40 = 34
    • Final Rating: 48 + 34 = 82
  5. Ensuring Transparency and Fairness: To promote transparency, both managers' evaluations were made accessible to the employee. This allowed employees to understand their strengths and areas for improvement from different perspectives. Additionally, any discrepancies in the ratings were discussed and resolved through a structured review process.
  6. Continuous Improvement: We also established a feedback loop to continuously improve the appraisal process. Regular feedback from managers and employees was collected and analysed to make necessary adjustments to the evaluation criteria, weightage, and overall process.

By implementing this comprehensive approach, we ensured that the performance appraisal process was fair, unbiased, and reflective of the employee's true performance. This not only helped in identifying top performers but also in providing constructive feedback for professional development.

 

Configuration Steps

To achieve the desired result in SuccessFactors, we followed a structured configuration process, solution involves creating a new route map, defining business rules, and configuring a performance form to achieve the desired result. Below are the detailed steps:

Step 1: Create a New Route Map

To start, we created a new route map where both the manager (EM) and the second manager (EMM) hold distinct steps. This setup ensures that each role has a designated step in the appraisal process, allowing their respective ratings to be considered in the overall calculation.

Step 2: Define Business Rules

Next, we created two business rules to facilitate the weighted rating system:

  • Trigger Weighted Rating Rule: This rule triggers at a specific step in the route map.
  • Configure Weight for Steps and Roles: This rule defines the percentage of the overall rating that each role contributes.
    • Screenshot 1

    • Screenshot 2

    • Screenshot 3

    • Screenshot 4

These rules ensure that the ratings provided by the manager and the second manager are considered according to the configured weights.

Step 3: Create a Performance Form

We then created a performance form and added a section named "Customized Weighted Rating." This section is configured to calculate the overall rating based on the ratings provided by both the manager and the second manager. The permissions are set to ensure that both roles can input their ratings against competencies and objectives.

Expected Result

With this configuration, both the manager and the second manager will provide ratings. These ratings will be calculated in the "Customized Weighted Rating" section according to the percentages specified in the business rules. This approach ensures a balanced and comprehensive performance appraisal process.

Summary

By implementing a weighted rating system in SuccessFactors, organizations can achieve a more balanced and comprehensive performance appraisal process. This approach ensures that performance evaluations are fair and reflect a broader perspective. Incorporating feedback from both the manager and the second manager enhances the accuracy of the overall rating and promotes a more inclusive appraisal culture. Continuous improvement and feedback loops further refine the process, ensuring its effectiveness and relevance in the ever-changing organizational landscape.

entry.title: SAP B1 Check batch or S/n status with TransacNotification on PickList
entry.content:
Hello, 

I'm trying to block the creation of a picklist when a batch or a serial number has the status 'Locked' (it's a ZU). I've already done it for sales orders, delivery, but for picklists I can't make the link between OPKL and OBTN/OSRN. I try to use OITL and ITL1 like for sales orders or delivery but there is no docType 156 inside OITL and ITL1

SELECT count(*)
FROM OPKL t0
JOIN PKL1 t1 ON t1."AbsEntry"= t0."AbsEntry"
JOIN OITL ON oitl."DocType"=156 AND oitl."DocEntry" =t1."AbsEntry" AND oitl."DocLine"=t1."OrderLine"
JOIN ITL1 ON oitl."LogEntry" = itl1."LogEntry"
JOIN OSRN t3 ON t3."ItemCode"=itl1."ItemCode" AND t3."SysNumber" = itl1."SysNumber"
WHERE t1."AbsEntry"=list_of_cols_val_tab_del AND t3."U_QC_STATUS" = 'LOCKED' ;

Thank you for your help,

Regards,

Antony

entry.title: Mass Update of Max Stock Level from Excel File Using MM46 in SAP S/4HANA Retail
entry.content:
In this case, we need to update the Max Stock Level (MABST) in the MARC table of SAP S/4HANA Retail using transaction MM46. To achieve this, we will prepare an Excel file with the required data and import it into SAP. Below are the steps to perform this update efficiently.


Step 1: Identify the Tables and Fields to Update

For this case, we need to update:

  • Table: MARC (Plant Data for Material)
  • Field: MABST (Max Stock Level)

Step 2: Prepare an Excel File (.XLSX)

Create an Excel file with three columns:

  • Material (MATNR)
  • Plant (WERKS)
  • Max Stock Level (MABST)

Important: The first row should contain the technical field names to match the SAP system requirements. (See image below for reference).

Step 3: Execute Transaction MM46

  • Open SAP GUI and run Transaction MM46.
  • Select the table (MARC) and the fields that need to be updated.

Step 4: Import the Excel File

  • Click on the "Import" button.
  • Select the prepared Excel file (.XLSX).
  • Ensure the system correctly maps the fields.

Step 5: Execute the Update

  • Confirm the settings to ensure the upload includes the header line (first row with technical field names).
  • Click on the "Execute" button.

Step 6: Preview Imported Data from Excel

  • Observe the uploaded data to ensure all fields are correctly populated.
  • Click on the "Execute" button.

Step 7: Confirm Imported Data on the Standard Screen

  • Observe the imported data to ensure accuracy.
  • Click on the "Test" button to validate the entries before finalizing the update.

Step 8: Review Results

  • Observe the results of the mass update.
  • Check the log for any errors or successful updates to ensure the process was completed correctly.

Conclusion

Using MM46 simplifies the mass update of material master data, saving time and ensuring data accuracy. This method is especially useful in SAP S/4HANA Retail, where frequent stock level adjustments are required.

entry.title: ABAP AUTHORITY CHECK Explained: A Practical Guide with Real-World Example
entry.content:
AUTHORITY-CHECK IN ABAP: 

Is a concept which enables or allow users to perform certain functions/ activities in the SAP system 

So, we will look for an example of such activity: 

To create a material or sales order I suppose to have authorization  

System checks whether I am authorized for the task or not –then only the system will proceed further  

Suppose in a general scenario while talking a leave the manager needs to authorize the request of our leave approval . 

 

What happens in SAP ? 

In SAP basis person will assign the authorization object to user id to proceed it further  

 

Terminologies in authority-check. 

  • Object class: it is a container for authorization objects  
  • Authorization object: object to check authorization 
  • Authorization field: an authorization object has the authorization field which is used to define field values 

Here the hierarchy goes like  

 

  

 

T-CODES: 

  • Authorization field – SU20 
  • Object class and authorization object: SU21 

 

OVERVIEW: 

The first and foremost thing is we need to check for authorization object class 

Which is a container for authorization object  

Once we create the object --> we need to assign the filed 

 

 

Scenario: 

Suppose we are using MM01 transaction to create a material, and we get an error  

At this moment we can inform BASIS people to check for the authorization in case we need any. 

 

Step; 

  1. Create a material and put external BP  

 

 

 2.Put breakpoint on statement AUTHORITY-CHECK .  

  • Here we can see auth object is ‘M_MATE_NEW’ 
  • Field – ‘DUMMY' 
  • Value - ‘*’ 

 

 

 

3.If we provide the field in SU20 

 

 

 

  1. Whenever we execute sy-subrc will be zero – indicates that we are authorized 

Or else it will give sy-subrc  12 indicating we are not authorized to create the material 

 

 

 

  1. here we have provided the field ACTVT—We shall have a glance about this 

 

 

 

The filed ACTVT has some permitted values—click on the status icon beside ACTVT 

 

 

 

 

01- IT IS CREATE 

02- CHANGE 

03- DISPLAY 

 

  1. Suppose if we provide a material and put external bp –we get same authorization value 

 

 

 

7.So, in the ACTVT we saw 02- for change and same for display 

The ACTVT holds value-03 and here we can see the field value 

 

 

 

 

Now let's take real time scenario:  

Implementing authorization checks in ABAP program is crucial to ensure that only authorized users can perform operations like INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE on your cylinder data, which includes delivery charges, stock, and cylinder types. Here's how you can set up and demonstrate an AUTHORITY-CHECK based on the cylinder batch ID: 

 

Steps: 

  1. Create authorization object class in SU21 

 

 

 

 

 

 

2.Save it 

 

 

 3.Crate the authorization field 

 

 

 4.Select the operation you want to do on DB  

 

.  

 5.Select the activity and save it  

 

 

 Scenario – in the report we have stock table and particular user can delete update insert Once we are done with auth object—basis person will create a role using t code pfcg 

 

  1. If we goto to the t code SU01 – AND CLICK ON role we can be able to see the assigned roles 

 

 

  1. Authorization filed and value will be assigned by BASIS and will reflect here  

After assigning role 

 

 

   code  :

 

 

 

 

 

 

REPORT zpd_rp_auth_chek. 
 
TYPES : BEGIN OF lty_BID, 
          cylinder_batch_id TYPE zpd_de_batch_id, 
        END OF lty_BID. 
 
DATA : lwa_data TYPE zpd_t_stock. 
DATA : zpd_de_batch_id TYPE TABLE OF lty_BID. 
DATA : lwa_bid TYPE  lty_BID. 
DATA : lt_bid TYPE  TABLE OF lty_bid. 
 
 
TYPES : BEGIN OF lty_display, 
          delivery_charge TYPE zpd_de_charge, 
          cylinder_type   TYPE zpd_de_cylinder_type, 
          stock           TYPE zpd_de_stock, 
        END OF lty_display. 
 
DATA : lwa_display TYPE lty_display. 
DATA : lv_objectid TYPE cdhdr-objectid. 
*DATA : lwa_bid TYPE zpd_t_stock. 
 
 
 
PARAMETERS : p_id TYPE zpd_de_batch_id OBLIGATORY. 
PARAMETERS : p_charge TYPE  zpd_de_charge  . 
PARAMETERS : p_type TYPE zpd_de_cylinder_type . 
PARAMETERS : p_stock TYPE  zpd_de_stock MODIF ID a3. 
PARAMETERS : p_r1 TYPE c RADIOBUTTON GROUP r1  USER-COMMAND abc . 
PARAMETERS : p_r2 TYPE c RADIOBUTTON GROUP r1 . 
PARAMETERS : p_r3 TYPE c RADIOBUTTON GROUP r1.. 
 
 
 
START-OF-SELECTION. 
 
*Insert 
  IF p_r1 = 'X'. 
 
    AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'ZAUTHBID' 
ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '01'. 
  ENDIF. 
  IF sy-subrc <> 0 . 
    MESSAGE e005(zmsg_auth). 
 
    lwa_data-cylinder_batch_id =  p_id. 
    lwa_data-delivery_charge =  p_charge. 
    lwa_data-cylinder_type =  p_type. 
    lwa_data-stock =  p_stock. 
 
    INSERT zpd_t_stock FROM lwa_data. 
    IF sy-subrc = 0. 
      WRITE : TEXT-000. 
    ELSE. 
      WRITE : TEXT-001. 
    ENDIF. 
  ENDIF. 
 
 
 
*Delete. 
  IF p_r2 = 'X'. 
    AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'ZAUTHBID' 
ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '02'. 
  ENDIF. 
  IF sy-subrc <> 0 . 
    MESSAGE e007(zmsg_auth). 
 
 
 
    SELECT SINGLE * FROM zpd_t_stock INTO lwa_data WHERE cylinder_batch_id = p_id. 
    lwa_data-cylinder_batch_id = p_id. 
    DELETE zpd_t_stock FROM lwa_data. 
    IF sy-subrc = 0. 
      WRITE : TEXT-003 , ' :' , p_id. 
    ENDIF. 
  ENDIF. 
 
 
*Update 
  IF p_r3 = 'X'. 
 
    AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'ZAUTHBID' 
ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '06'. 
  ENDIF. 
  IF sy-subrc <> 0 . 
    MESSAGE e006(zmsg_auth). 
 
 
    lwa_data-cylinder_batch_id = p_id. 
    lwa_data-delivery_charge = p_charge. 
    lwa_data-cylinder_type = p_type. 
    lwa_data-stock = p_stock. 
 
    UPDATE zpd_t_stock FROM lwa_data. 
    IF sy-subrc = 0. 
      WRITE : TEXT-004, ' :' , p_id. 
    ENDIF. 
  ENDIF. 
 
 
 
 
 
AT SELECTION-SCREEN. 
  IF p_r1 = 'X'. 
    SELECT cylinder_batch_id 
    FROM zpd_t_stock 
    INTO TABLE lt_bid 
    WHERE cylinder_batch_id = p_id. 
 
    IF sy-subrc = 0. 
      MESSAGE e003(zmsg_auth) WITH p_id. 
    ENDIF. 
 
  ENDIF. 
 
  IF p_r2 = 'X'. 
 
    SELECT SINGLE cylinder_batch_id 
    FROM zpd_t_stock 
    INTO lwa_bid 
    WHERE cylinder_batch_id = p_id. 
    IF sy-subrc NE 0. 
      MESSAGE e004(zmsg_auth) WITH p_id. 
    ENDIF. 
  ENDIF. 
 
  IF p_r3 = 'X'. 
 
    SELECT SINGLE cylinder_batch_id 
    FROM zpd_t_stock 
    INTO lwa_bid 
    WHERE cylinder_batch_id = p_id. 
    IF sy-subrc NE 0. 
      MESSAGE e004(zmsg_auth) WITH p_id. 
    ELSE. 
      SELECT SINGLE   delivery_charge  stock 
      FROM zpd_t_stock 
      INTO lwa_display 
      WHERE cylinder_batch_id  = p_id. 
    ENDIF. 
  ENDIF. 
 
 
AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT. 
 
 
 
  LOOP AT SCREEN. 
    IF screen-group1 = 'A1' OR screen-group1 = 'A2'  OR screen-group1 = 'A3' OR screen-group1 = 'A4'. 
      screen-active = 0. 
      MODIFY SCREEN. 
    ENDIF. 
 
  ENDLOOP. 
 
 
  IF p_r3 = 'X'. 
    p_type = lwa_display-cylinder_type. 
    p_charge = lwa_display-delivery_charge. 
    p_stock = lwa_display-stock. 
 
  ENDIF.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Same in case of  delete .. 

 

 

So now lets dicuss about the key parameters present in the authorization  

SAP_ALL : 

  • The composite profile SAP_ALL contains all SAP authorizations 
  • It means that a user with this profile can perform all tasks in the SAP system  
  • The user which is having SAP_ALL authorization has the rights to administer the SAP system  
  • In projects, it is recommended to have only one user with SAP_ALL authorizations, rest all users have the authorization based upon their role 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Here I have authorization for SAP_ALL means I have sap all system authorization 

IN CASE: 

Even though I have SAP_ALL authorizations .then why is it not possible to create the records in previous program? 

 

When we created object, we need to add that new object in SAP_ALL 

And SAP_ALL has all many predefined authorizations 

To add that we need to regenerate SAP_ALL 

 

 

 

 

 

So once we regenerate it – it will be the part of SAP_ALL 

 

Now if we try to insert a record it will show – sy-subrc  will be 0 

 

 

 

 

 

  • Now let's see the requirement of assigning authorization for a particular field 

Suppose I want to assign auth for a custom field delivery charge – only certain people can  see the delivery  charges change the delivery charges 

 

  1. I have a custom table of cylinder data  

 

 

 2.Su20 – create field 

 

 

 3.Su21 create auth obj class—and use already existing class 

 

 

 

4.Here I'm providing authorization on displaying the details of delivery charges 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 Code : 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

TYPES : BEGIN OF lty_display, 
          delivery_charge TYPE zpd_de_charge, 
          cylinder_type   TYPE zpd_de_cylinder_type, 
          stock           TYPE zpd_de_stock, 
        END OF lty_display. 
 
 
DATA : lt_data TYPE TABLE OF lty_display, 
       wa      TYPE lty_display. 
 
PARAMETERS : p_dc TYPE zpd_de_stock. 
 
START-OF-SELECTION. 
  SELECT delivery_charge 
          cylinder_type 
           stock FROM zpd_t_stock 
  INTO TABLE lt_data 
   WHERE delivery_charge = p_dc . 
 
  LOOP AT lt_data INTO wa. 
    WRITE : wa-delivery_charge , wa-cylinder_type , wa-stock . 
 
  ENDLOOP. 
 
  AT SELECTION-SCREEN . 
   AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT  'ZDELICHRG' 
   ID 'ACTVT' FIELD '03' 
   ID 'DELIVCHARG' FIELD P_DC. 
   IF SY-SUBRC <> 0. 
     MESSAGE e008(ZMSG_AUTH) WITH p_dc. 
     ENDIF.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 5.We can see that we are getting sy-subrc = 12 

 

 

 

 6.So when we try to display the records with particular delivery charge we get an error 

 

 

 

 

Here are some simple conclusion points: 

  • Secure Data Access: Using AUTHORITY-CHECK ensures that only authorized users can update, delete, or insert cylinder data. 
  • Granular Permissions: Instead of giving broad access (like SAP_ALL), you assign rights for specific fields (like a cylinder batch ID) to limit who can change data. 
  • Role-Based Control: By creating roles in PFCG with precise authorization objects, you control access based on business needs. 
  • Risk Reduction: Fine-grained authorizations help reduce the risk of accidental or unauthorized data changes. 
  • Ongoing Review: Regularly reviewing and updating roles ensures that permissions stay relevant and secure.

entry.title: Free SAP BTP - Create new Subaccount
entry.content:
This article will show the creation of a new Subaccount in your recent SAP BTP global.

In order to create a new Subaccount, you need to be at the global account level (green rectangle) and then open the Create menu, selecting "Subaccount" (yellow ellipse).

A new screen will open, asking for important information about the new Subaccount, pay attention to the Region.

The region will have a BIG influence on what you can do (for free) in your SAP BTP account, I recommend taking a look at this link below to find out what are free services available in the region.

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/viewServices

In the spoiler there is a small branch in the subject, but necessary for future peace of mind.... feel free to sneak peek 

Spoiler

There is a service called "Landscape Portal" responsible for admin tasks at SAP BTP, like Hana hibernation, support users, etc... I found out the hard way that this service is only available in EUR (cf-eu10 or cf-eu11), after getting crazy looking the region I choose ( Azure US East).

My advice is this service will be used every day to start the Hana system (I will explain why in the next article), this switch takes only 3 clicks. For us developers who are concerned about the new development performance, LAG, and possible expansion, look for the region closest to you. I just created another Subaccount in eu10 and everything else close to me in USA/Texas.

After the SAP BTP process the creation of your new Subaccount, you can go into it and create the new services (remember to choose free ones).

Also, now is a good time to back to the main global account and take a look into the finance part (Cost and Usage).

Here you can check and fix potential cost associate to you account. We are trying to stay at free tier / no-cost.

If you missed the 1st article how to create your global account, you can navigate to it below or go to next article showing how to create / deploy your first service.

entry.title: Beyond SAP BW: Unlocking Agility and Innovation with the Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
The Modern Data Imperative

The business landscape has fundamentally transformed, creating new demands on enterprise data platforms:
  • Real-time analytics has replaced batch processing as organizations require immediate insights to respond to market dynamics
  • Data diversity continues to expand beyond SAP systems to include SaaS applications, IoT devices, and external sources
  • Advanced analytics capabilities including AI and machine learning have shifted from competitive advantages to operational necessities
  • Cloud-native scalability has become essential for adapting to fluctuating workloads and evolving business requirements
 

Strategic Benefits of Migrating to the Business Data Cloud

The Business Data Cloud delivers substantial advantages for organizations seeking to modernize their data infrastructure:
  • Accelerated decision-making through near real-time data processing and analysis
  • Comprehensive data integration creates a unified enterprise view across disparate sources
  • Enhanced analytical capabilities leveraging built-in AI and machine learning capabilities
  • Dynamic scalability that adapts to changing business requirements without infrastructure constraints
  • Improved business agility through accelerated deployments and rapid insight generation
  • Future-ready architecture built on modern, cloud-native principles that support ongoing innovation
 

Migration Pathways: A Structured Approach

Transitioning from SAP BW to the Business Data Cloud follows distinct paths based on your current environment. Each approach is designed to minimize disruption while maximizing the benefits of modernization.
 
BW to BDC: A Visual Pathway

 

For BW Systems on Non-HANA Databases

BW on any DB pathway

 

Initial Requirement: Migration to HANA
The foundation of your BDC journey begins with moving to the SAP HANA database platform, which provides the in-memory architecture necessary for real-time analytics.

Post-HANA Options:
  • BW 7.5 on HANA: A targeted upgrade that preserves existing functionality while enabling HANA-specific capabilities
  • BW/4HANA 2023: A comprehensive modernization that delivers the latest BW capabilities and prepares your environment for better BDC integration
 

For BW Systems Already on HANA

NetWeaver Version Assessment
BW on HANA pathway

 

  • NetWeaver < 7.5: Options include upgrading to NetWeaver 7.5 (SPS24 or greater) as a minimum requirement or migrating directly to BW/4HANA 2023
  • NetWeaver 7.5: Ensure Service Pack level meets minimum requirements (SPS24)
  • Direct Path: Regardless of the current NetWeaver version, migration to BW/4HANA 2023 remains available as a comprehensive modernization option
 
For Existing BW/4HANA Environments
BW/4HANA pathway

 

  • BW/4HANA 1.0 or 2.0: Upgrade to BW/4HANA 2023 for latest capabilities
  • BW/4HANA 2021: Apply the latest Service Pack or upgrade to BW/4HANA 2023
  • BW/4HANA 2023: Implement the most recent Service Pack to ensure optimal performance and feature availability

 

Addressing Enterprise Concerns

Migration projects involve careful consideration of several key factors:
  • Assessment of Current Environment: Thoroughly assess the existing infrastructure, applications, and data. This helps understand the scope of the migration and identify any potential challenges.
  • Planning and Strategy: Develop a detailed migration plan that includes timelines, resource allocation, and risk management strategies. A well-defined strategy ensures a smooth transition with minimal disruptions.
  • Data Integrity and Security: Ensure that data integrity is maintained throughout the migration process. Implement robust security measures to protect sensitive information from breaches or loss.
  • Compatibility and Interoperability: Verify that the new environment is compatible with existing systems and applications. Address any interoperability issues to ensure seamless integration.
  • Testing and Validation: Conduct thorough testing and validation at each stage of the migration. This helps identify and resolve issues early, ensuring a successful migration.
  • User Training and Support: Provide adequate training and support to users to help them adapt to the new environment. This minimizes resistance and ensures a smooth transition.
 

Strategic Assessment Questions

  • Before embarking on your migration journey, consider these fundamental questions:
  • What specific data management limitations are you currently experiencing with your BW environment?
  • Which analytical capabilities would deliver the most significant business impact?
  • How do your current data integration processes affect business agility?
  • What performance improvements would most benefit your decision-making processes?
  • What integration requirements exist between your data platform and other enterprise systems?
  • How does your BDC migration align with broader digital transformation initiatives?
 
Many talk about data-driven decisions, but few truly execute. Are you ready to move beyond theory and into action? The power is in your hands.

entry.title: Teaching Business Process Simulation and Sustainability in Procurement
entry.content:
Companies need to continuously evaluate and optimize their business processes and ensure critical supply chain operations. As a result, Business Process Management (BPM) has become integral to both academic curricula and industry practice. Yet, while BPM techniques like process modeling and mining are well-established in education, business process simulation is often overlooked. This blog post explores a new approach to teaching process simulation within the context of sustainability in procurement, leveraging SAP Signavio to enrich student learning.

SAP Signavio Process Simulation Curriculum

Business process simulation—analyzing “what-if” scenarios to optimize operations—helps students and professionals gain insights into process efficiency, bottlenecks, and optimization strategies, making it a critical yet underutilized tool in BPM education.

To foster data-driven decision making and process simulation skills, we developed a process simulation case study that focuses on sustainability in procurement.* Using SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite, academic edition, our case study extends an existing ERP simulation teaching case by adding an extra subprocess for supplier selection:

Sustainable Procurement Case Study

In our teaching case, A Company that Makes Apparel (ACM Apparel) is navigating new European Union regulations on sustainability due diligence in supply chains. The case walks learners through the traditional procure-to-pay (P2P) process and then challenges them to optimize this process with sustainability and efficiency considerations using IT systems. Students engage with real-world scenarios and develop process optimization skills crucial in today’s fast-paced business environment.

Hands-On Learning with SAP Signavio

The simulation case is designed to be interactive and adaptable, allowing educators to adjust the complexity based on course needs. Students begin by modeling a manual supplier selection process, learning about the S2P and P2P processes using a real-world scenario. They then simulate their supplier selection process using SAP Signavio, comparing the outcomes of different scenarios to identify inefficiencies.

While the sustainability due diligence increases the time and cost of supplier selection, students are guided to re-engineer this the process, automating and integrating process steps using SAP Ariba and SAP Business Network. This reduces costs and ensures compliance with sustainability standards.

Get started with SAP Signavio Simulation

If you’re and educator wanting to explore SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite for education, you can register for an unlimited free academic workspace using your university e-mail address at academic.signavio.com.

If you want to start teaching sustainability and process re-enigneering using simulation in SAP Signavio, you can download the teaching slide deck. In case of questions, feel free to reach out to signavio.academics@sap.com!

You can find the complete case description in the paper "Teaching Business Process Simulation and Sustainability in Procurement" that was published in the conference proceedings of the 2024 SAP Academic Community Conference D-A-CH.

*The curriculum has been validated by industry practitioners and tested in educational settings, showing significant potential in enhancing BPM education.
